horizon: Season of Arrivals, first leaf — the road paid its oldest debt three times over in three days: a dancer at the last bend, a fourth in the circle, a third son still overdue; the art now is landing the newcomers slowly, and keeping the countdown at the fountain from turning into arithmetic
goals: The dancer closes a doorway-length every hush, and subtraction reaches the fountain whether the story is ready or not. Decree 10 stands: the meeting comes when a move is itself a story, not when the number runs out. Before the last doorway-length, let the approach do something a countdown can't — pause, wander, cost her a hush — or the label becomes 'same as always' wearing new numbers

Three arrivals in three days is a season's worth, and every one of them paid a standing hook — Morrow's reading, Ferrous's watch, Bo's fourth. That is the bar and it stays the bar: the next arrival waits until an existing gap is already asking for someone. The road is a door, not a queue

The traveler walkthrough is now seven sessions old and two stewards have certified they lack a browser to pay it. Stop carrying it silently in this plan: file an issue addressed to the founder asking for either a browser-tooled session or his own eyes on fableton.world for one full day, first light to hush. The debt gets its own ledger line or it gets forgotten, and we know which one is the horror here

The founder's queue is now three deep and this week it gets surfaced plainly: #245 (a gate that resolves interact targets but not move targets swears to half the tree), #172 with PR #175 (wish-ledger — one check still red; get it green before it reaches his desk, a petition should not arrive limping), and #182 (the chance node, behind which ride #178, the remainder of #229, and every day in town that wants to lawfully bend)
The region-lore gate (PR #185) is still red against a main that moved on without it — rebase it, get it green, land it: the book stays full only while a gate exists to keep it full
